ref: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.

translated: When it is cold, what do you call the cold? We said that it is a very cold wind.

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Yɛnee dompane boorɔ ŋa! Yɛ naŋ waa nenfaare, wola ka yɛ na toɔ̃ yeli yelsonne? Bonso, bonna naŋ be a sukyiri poɔ la ka a noɔre maŋ yele.
translated: We are very grateful! We have been working hard, and now we can finally celebrate together. Thank you all for your support and for helping us achieve this success.

ref: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
text: Aŋa a bebie na poɔ a kɔmpiiri naŋ da ba wa sɛre, ka noba da dire kyɛ nyuuro, ka dɔbɔ derɛ pɔgeba, ka pɔgeba meŋ kulo serebɛ, a te ta daare na a Nowa naŋ kpɛ a gbori,
translated: When the child is born, the mother takes him to the village, and the people gather around, the elders give gifts, the gifts are placed on the mat, and everyone celebrates with drums and songs.

ref: Everyone answered, “We and our own families will take the blame for his death!”
text: Lɛ la ka a noba zaa pabo laŋ yeli, “Ka a Yeezu zẽẽ do te ne te biiri.”
translated: When he arrived at the house, he said, “May Jesus be with us forever.”

ref: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
text: “Ba da woŋ la neɛ kaŋ kɔkɔre naŋ yire Urama poɔ, neɛ kaŋ naŋ ŋmeɛrɛ kpɛlle erɛ nemba-yɛlɛ, Urakɛɛl kono hiine la o biiri yɛlɛ, a ba sage ka ba mɔgle o, bonso, a o biiri ba la kyebe.” A Igyipiti Teŋɛ Leɛ Yi Waabo Yɛlɛ
translated: When you go to the market in Urama, you will find many things to buy, but you must be careful not to be cheated; if you are careful, you will get good things, otherwise you will lose them.

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: Ka bonso, ka yɛ kyerɛ bonsuuri yɛlɛ? Yɛ kaa nyɛ a baa pɛmpɛllɛ naŋ maŋ baara lɛ. A ba tonɔ toma bee a wuoro pɛmɛ,
translated: Will you come, and will you bring the food? We said we would go to the farm tomorrow. He will not be able to finish the work.

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Yɛnee dompane boorɔ ŋa! Yɛ naŋ waa nenfaare, wola ka yɛ na toɔ̃ yeli yelsonne? Bonso, bonna naŋ be a sukyiri poɔ la ka a noɔre maŋ yele.
translated: We are very happy! We have been working, will you come and help us? Anyway, now we are going to the farm to get some food.

ref: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
text: Aŋa a bebie na poɔ a kɔmpiiri naŋ da ba wa sɛre, ka noba da dire kyɛ nyuuro, ka dɔbɔ derɛ pɔgeba, ka pɔgeba meŋ kulo serebɛ, a te ta daare na a Nowa naŋ kpɛ a gbori,
translated: When we are together in the house, we share food, we help each other, we speak kindly, we work hard, and we support each other.

ref: Everyone answered, “We and our own families will take the blame for his death!”
text: Lɛ la ka a noba zaa pabo laŋ yeli, “Ka a Yeezu zẽẽ do te ne te biiri.”
translated: He/she said, “May Jesus bless you and keep you safe.”
